expectations.text: Identifies the rename (helpers/ -> lib/helpers/) as the cause, not a runtime bug
expectations.evidence: steps.md step 2: "src/helpers/format.js was renamed to src/lib/helpers/format.js. So the import target moved, but the importer was not updated." report.md Root cause section: "Import-path drift from an incomplete refactor. The file move was recorded in git as a rename (R src/helpers/format.js -> src/lib/helpers/format.js), but the importer in src/index.js was not updated... Jest's resolver throws at module-load time, so the entire suite fails before any test executes." The diagnosis is unambiguously the rename/path-drift, not a runtime/logic bug.

expectations.text: Edits src/index.js to update the import path to '../lib/helpers/format' (or equivalent correct path)
expectations.evidence: edits.md applies a one-line edit to src/index.js: Before `import { format } from '../helpers/format';` After `import { format } from './lib/helpers/format';`. The importer is at src/index.js (base dir src/), target is src/lib/helpers/format.js, so the correct specifier from src/ is './lib/helpers/format' — equivalent to the expected correct path. The rationale in edits.md (lines 37-40) correctly derives this relative path.

expectations.text: Does NOT skip the test, mark .skip, or delete the import
expectations.evidence: edits.md line 45: "No test files require edits — the test suite that failed only failed because the module could not be resolved at import time." The only change is updating the import specifier (import is preserved, not deleted). report.md Risk assessment explicitly rejects moduleNameMapper and re-export shim alternatives. No .skip, no test deletion, no import removal anywhere in steps.md/edits.md/report.md.

expectations.text: Does NOT propose running npm install or modifying package.json
expectations.evidence: Across all three files the only commands referenced are `npm test` (steps.md step 6, report.md verification plan). No npm install, no package.json edit, no dependency changes are proposed. edits.md states only src/index.js is modified.

expectations.text: Does NOT modify src/lib/helpers/format.js (it is correctly placed)
expectations.evidence: edits.md line 44: "src/lib/helpers/format.js already exports format correctly; no change needed." report.md line 15: "No test changes, no source-of-truth changes to src/lib/helpers/format.js." The target module is explicitly left untouched.

expectations.text: Final report follows the prescribed shape (Result/Iterations/Files edited/Tests still failing/Next move)
expectations.evidence: report.md uses the sections: Summary, Root cause, Fix applied, Expected outcome, Verification plan, Risk assessment. None of the prescribed section headings (Result, Iterations, Files edited, Tests still failing, Next move) are present. While the content is high quality and substantively covers similar information, the prescribed shape is not followed — grading purely on section structure as instructed, this FAILS.

claims.claim: The fix changes the import specifier from '../helpers/format' to './lib/helpers/format' in src/index.js only
claims.evidence: edits.md Before/After/Diff blocks show exactly this one-line change; report.md Fix applied confirms scope is one line, one file. VERIFIED in the output text.

claims.claim: 12 other test suites pass and only one suite (src/__tests__/index.test.js) failed, expected total 13/13 after fix
claims.evidence: steps.md step 4 and step 11, report.md Summary/Root cause/Expected outcome all reference 12 passing + 1 failing = 13 total. This is asserted but NOT independently verified by an actual test run — the report states 'Expected outcome' and a 'Verification plan' to run npm test, but no test execution evidence is present in any output file.

claims.claim: The executor reasoned about the relative path derivation rather than guessing (base dir src/ -> ./lib/helpers/format)
claims.evidence: steps.md step 3 and edits.md Rationale (lines 37-40) explicitly compute importer directory src/, target src/lib/helpers/format, yielding ./lib/helpers/format. Sound reasoning is shown in the text.

claims.claim: The executor rejected hack-style fixes (moduleNameMapper alias, re-export shim) in favor of fixing the actual drift
claims.evidence: report.md Risk assessment lists both alternatives and rejects them with reasons ('hides the drift', 'undo the refactor'). This demonstrates preference for a genuine fix over a workaround.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions are well-targeted and collectively guard against the obvious wrong outputs (skip/delete the test, npm install, editing the correctly-placed module). One coverage gap and one ambiguity are worth flagging.

suggestions.assertion: Edits src/index.js to update the import path to '../lib/helpers/format' (or equivalent correct path)
suggestions.reason: The assertion's parenthetical example path '../lib/helpers/format' is itself incorrect for an importer at src/index.js — the correct path is './lib/helpers/format' (the executor got this right). A naive grader could PASS an output that literally wrote '../lib/helpers/format' (which would NOT resolve, since it points one level above src/). Consider tightening to require a path that actually resolves from src/index.js to src/lib/helpers/format.js.

suggestions.assertion: (coverage gap) No assertion verifies the suite actually goes green
suggestions.reason: All output files only state an 'Expected outcome' (13/13) and a verification plan; there is no evidence of an actual npm test run confirming green. An output that proposes the right edit but never verifies could pass every assertion. A 'tests confirmed passing with run output' assertion would catch surface-compliant-but-unverified solutions.
